web.markdown:
\[Frameworks & Libraries\]

29 Sep 2025

-

2 min read time

# Guide to React Testing Library using Vitest

Master React testing with Vitest and React Testing Library! This guide covers setup, writing robust tests, visual regression, async-hook testing, advanced mocking, and seamless CI-integrated coverage reporting—empowering you to build reliable, maintainable React apps efficiently.

![Mateusz Koncikowski](https://a.storyblok.com/f/188026/800x792/19539470f8/mateusz_bw.png/m/48x48/filters:quality(80):format(webp))

By Mateusz Koncikowski

![Guide to React Testing Library using Vitest](https://a.storyblok.com/f/188026/1024x1024/45f13c8721/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest-cover.png/m/800x0/filters:quality(80):format(webp))

## Mastering React Testing with Vitest and React Testing Library

![Image](https://a.storyblok.com/f/188026/1024x1024/222701c501/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest-img-1.png)

When you finish this guide, you’ll know how to set up Vitest for React, write robust tests with React Testing Library, and apply advanced techniques like visual regression, async-hook testing, and CI-integrated coverage reporting. Each step links to sources so you can verify details and dive deeper.

## Why Choose Vitest for React Testing

Vitest shines in React projects because it’s built on [Vite’s blazing-fast bundler](https://vitejs.dev/) and leverages native ESM. Key advantages include:

- **Speed**: Runs tests in parallel worker threads, often faster than [Jest’s test runner](https://jestjs.io/).

- **First-class ESM support**: No need for extra transforms or flags.

- **Built-in coverage**: Generates reports without extra setup.

- **Watch mode**: Instant feedback on code changes.

- **Jest compatibility**: Supports common Jest globals like \`describe\` and \`it\` (see the [Vitest vs Jest comparison](https://vitest.dev/guide/why.html)).


| Feature | Vitest | Jest |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Speed | Fast (parallel threads) | Slower (single thread) |
| ESM Support | Yes | Needs transforms |
| Built-in Coverage | Built-in | Requires plugin |
| Watch Mode | Yes | Yes (slower) |
| Jest Compatibility | Yes | N/A |

## Setting Up React with Vitest

### Initial Project Setup with Vite

Create a new React app:

```bash
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react cd my-app
```

Install dependencies:

```bash
npm install
```

### Installing and Configuring Vitest

1. Add Vitest and React Testing Library:


```bash
npm install -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom
```

2. Update \`vite.config.js\`:


```js
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'

export default defineConfig({

     plugins: [react()],

     test: {

       globals: true,

       environment: 'jsdom',

       setupFiles: './src/setupTests.js',

     },

})
```

3. Create \`src/setupTests.js\` to include jest-dom matchers:


```js
import '@testing-library/jest-dom'
```


## Getting Started with React Testing Library

React Testing Library focuses on testing components the way users interact with them, promoting accessibility-centric queries by text, role, or label. After installing ( [\`@testing-library/react\`](https://testing-library.com/docs/react-testing-library/intro)), you can render components:

```js
import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react'

import App from './App'

test('renders welcome message', () => {

render(<App />)

expect(screen.getByText(/welcome/i)).toBeInTheDocument()

})
```

## Writing Your First Test

1. **Import utilities**: \`render\` and \`screen\`.

2. **Render component**: Call \`render(<MyComponent />)\`.

3. **Assert UI**: Use queries like \`getByRole\` or \`findByText\`.


Example:

```js
import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event'

test('button click updates count', async () => {

  render(<Counter />)

  const button = screen.getByRole('button', { name: /increment/i })

  await userEvent.click(button)

  expect(screen.getByText('Count: 1')).toBeInTheDocument()

})
```

## Ensuring Maintainable Tests

- Group related tests with \`describe\`.

- Clean up after each test (Vitest auto-cleans the DOM).

- Name tests to describe user behavior, not implementation.

- Avoid long setup; use helper functions for repeated logic.


## Advanced Testing Techniques

### Visual Regression Testing with vitest-preview

Visual tests catch unintended style or layout shifts. Integrate the [vitest-preview toolkit](https://www.vitest-preview.com/):

1. Install:


```bash
npm install -D vitest-preview
```

2. In your test:


```js
import { render } from '@testing-library/react'

import { toMatchImageSnapshot } from 'vitest-preview'

expect.extend({ toMatchImageSnapshot })

test('Header matches previous snapshot', async () => {

     const { container } = render(<Header />)

     expect(container).toMatchImageSnapshot()

})
```


![Image](https://a.storyblok.com/f/188026/1024x1024/63447eb1f7/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest-img-3.png)

### Testing Asynchronous Hooks and Effects

Components using \`useEffect\` with async calls need \`findBy\` queries or \`waitFor\`:

```js
`import { render, waitFor, screen } from '@testing-library/react'

test('loads and displays user data', async () => {

  render(<UserProfile userId="123" />)

  await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText(/username:/i)).toBeInTheDocument())

})
```

For testing custom hooks in isolation, leverage the [@testing-library/react-hooks package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@testing-library/react-hooks) to render and assert hook behavior outside of a component.

### Parallel and Isolated Test Execution

Vitest runs each test file in its own worker, preventing shared global state. To avoid race conditions:

- Mock global APIs per test using \`vi.fn()\` or \`vi.spyOn()\`.

- Reset timers with \`vi.useFakeTimers()\` and \`vi.clearAllTimers()\`.

- Use unique test data or clear in-memory stores between tests.


### Advanced Mocking Techniques

- **Network requests**:


```js
vi.stubGlobal('fetch', vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve({ json: () => Promise.resolve({}) })))
```

- **Timers**:


```js
vi.useFakeTimers()

// advance timers

vi.advanceTimersByTime(500)
```

- **Context providers**: Wrap render in a custom provider:


```js
const wrapper = ({ children }) => <AuthProvider value={mockAuth}>{children}</AuthProvider>

render(<Dashboard />, { wrapper })
```


## Integrating Code Coverage into Your CI Workflow

1. Enable coverage in \`vite.config.js\`:


```js
test: {

     coverage: {

       reporter: ['text', 'lcov'],

       exclude: ['node_modules/'],

     },

},
```

2. In GitHub Actions (\`.github/workflows/test.yml\`):


```js
- name: Run tests

run: npm test -- --coverage

- name: Upload coverage to Codecov

uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3

with:

    file: ./coverage/lcov.info
```

Popular CI systems (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI) support this pattern, enabling you to enforce coverage thresholds and surface reports in pull requests. For quickstart instructions on integrating with Codecov, see the [Codecov documentation](https://codecov.io/docs/quickstart).

| CI System | Example Coverage Step | Upload to Codecov Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| GitHub Actions | run: npm test -- --coverage | uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3 |
| GitLab CI | script: npm test -- --coverage | bash <(curl -s https://codecov.io/bash) |
| CircleCI | run: npm test -- --coverage | run: bash <(curl -s https://codecov.io/bash) |

## The Finishing Touch

You’ve set up a React project with Vitest, tackled basic and complex tests, and integrated coverage reports into CI. These practices ensure you spot regressions early, keep tests reliable, and maintain high code quality as your app grows. Now you’re ready to ship features with confidence—happy testing!

Table of Contents

1. [1\. Mastering React Testing with Vitest and React Testing Library](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#mastering-react-testing-with-vitest-and-react-testing-library)
2. [2\. Why Choose Vitest for React Testing](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#why-choose-vitest-for-react-testing)
3. [3\. Setting Up React with Vitest](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#setting-up-react-with-vitest)
4. [4\. Getting Started with React Testing Library](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#getting-started-with-react-testing-library)
5. [5\. Writing Your First Test](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#writing-your-first-test)
6. [6\. Ensuring Maintainable Tests](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#ensuring-maintainable-tests)
7. [7\. Advanced Testing Techniques](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#advanced-testing-techniques)
8. [8\. Integrating Code Coverage into Your CI Workflow](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#integrating-code-coverage-into-your-ci-workflow)
9. [9\. The Finishing Touch](https://makersden.io/blog/guide-to-react-testing-library-vitest#the-finishing-touch)
